plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and comply with the single-name mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, aligning with capital preservation.
Reduce SMH to 10% due to its downtrend, further de-risking the portfolio.
Increase QQQI to 25% to enhance income and provide more stability.
Add XLV (Healthcare) at 5% to diversify the portfolio into a high-quality sector.
Maintain a significant cash buffer to absorb potential market shocks.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a modest ~1.5% monthly return, prioritizing capital preservation and low drawdown. It significantly reduces overall portfolio risk by eliminating leveraged ETFs and diversifying across sectors. Expect lower volatility, but also more limited upside potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single-name concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL due to its 'below stop' status, and reduce SPAL to 3% to limit leveraged ETF exposure.
Reduce SMH to 10% to decrease exposure to a downtrending sector.
Add LRCX and ASML, both high-scoring semiconductor setups, at 7% each to maintain tech exposure with stronger names.
This plan balances growth with risk management, maintaining a moderate cash buffer.
The overall portfolio will be diversified while leaning into quality tech setups.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for ~2.5% monthly growth, focusing on risk-first growth by reducing leverage and concentration. It maintains exposure to quality tech while managing downside. Expect moderate volatility and a balanced risk-reward profile.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single-name concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL due to its 'below stop' status, adhering to strict discipline.
Reduce SMH to 10% to decrease exposure to a downtrending sector.
Increase CRWD to 18% to press a strong uptrending name.
Buy LRCX and ASML at 10% each, leveraging high-scoring semiconductor setups.
Add SPAL to 5% to utilize leveraged ETFs within the mandate, increasing overall exposure.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% monthly growth by stretching into high-scoring setups and leveraging exposure. It carries significantly higher risk, including larger potential drawdowns and the daily decay/rebalancing effects of leveraged ETFs. Strict stop discipline is crucial to manage losses in this mode.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and shift towards income-generating assets.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to completely eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Sell SMH and reduce CRWD to 5% to minimize exposure to high-beta, growth-oriented names.
Significantly increase QQQI to 40% to maximize monthly distributions.
Reduce QQQ to 10% to further de-risk and prioritize income over growth.
Maintain a cash buffer for stability, focusing on low drawdown and reliable distributions.
income.read: This income plan targets a ~1% monthly distribution yield by rotating into income and covered-call ETFs. It prioritizes low drawdown and steady distributions over growth. While providing cash-like income, distributions are variable and NAV can still decline in market downturns, so it is not a savings account.
